name: Embalse de Orellana, castillo de Puebla de Alcocer y Cerro Masatrigo – circular desde la presa de La Serena
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embalse de la Serena offers diverse terrain characterized by the vast "inland sea" landscape of the reservoir, undulating plains, and unique geological features. The region, part of the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve, features extensive open spaces and hills, including the prominent conical shape of Cerro Masatrigo. Trails combine flat sections along the water's edge with more demanding routes that include significant ascents. This environment provides varied mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de la Serena The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Orellana, castillo de Puebla de Alcocer y Cerro Masatrigo – circular desde la presa de La Serena, a 48.1 miles (77.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route combines reservoir views with historical landmarks and the iconic Cerro Masatrigo. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Serena Reservoir and Cima de las Poyatas Loop, a difficult 36.3 miles (58.5 km) path. This route offers extensive views of the reservoir and includes significant elevation changes. Local mountain bikers also love the Cerro Cogolludo Tunnel – Playa de los Calicantos loop from Puebla de Alcocer, a 43.6 miles (70.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Cerro Cogolludo Tunnel and along the reservoir's edge, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de la Serena is defined by vast reservoir expanses, undulating plains, and the distinctive Cerro Masatrigo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Embalse de la Serena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 14 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de la Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embalse de la Serena?
A: There are 8 mountain bike trails available around Embalse de la Serena, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Embalse de la Serena?
A: The terrain around Embalse de la Serena is diverse, characterized by the vast "inland sea" landscape of the reservoir, undulating plains, and unique geological features. You'll find extensive open spaces, hills, and the prominent conical shape of Cerro Masatrigo. Trails combine flatter sections along the water's edge with more demanding routes that include significant ascents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Embalse de la Serena?
A: While many routes offer challenging ascents, some sections along the water's edge are less demanding and can be suitable for families seeking a relaxed pace with spectacular views. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mural of Rural Life – Puebla de Alcocer Castle loop from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.3 km long and takes about 2 hours 39 minutes.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or natural features to see while mountain biking?
A: A standout natural attraction is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, often called the "Magic Mountain" due to its almost perfectly conical shape, offering breathtaking 360-degree views. You can also see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zújar River&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes provide extensive views of the reservoir itself.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced riders, there are demanding routes with technical sections and pronounced ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032981767/embalse-de-orellana-castillo-de-puebla-de-alcocer-y-cerro-masatrigo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Orellana, castillo de Puebla de Alcocer y Cerro Masatrigo – circular desde la presa de La Serena&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 77.5 km trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, combining reservoir views with historical landmarks and the iconic Cerro Masatrigo.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Embalse de la Serena?
A: The region is part of Extremadura, which can experience very hot summers. Therefore, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and comfortable riding conditions. Winter can also be suitable, though some days might be cooler.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201089160/puebla-de-alcocer-to-cerro-masatrigo-and-embalse-de-la-serena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Alcocer to Cerro Masatrigo and Embalse de la Serena Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50 km circular route that takes about 4 hours 15 minutes to complete.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Embalse de la Serena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic views of the "inland sea" landscape, the unique geological features like Cerro Masatrigo, and the diverse trails that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural beauty with historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region offers routes that blend natural beauty with historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Cogolludo Tunnel – Playa de los Calicantos loop from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 70.1 km trail that leads through varied landscapes, including the Cerro Cogolludo Tunnel and along the reservoir's edge, offering a mix of nature and local points of interest.

Q: Is Embalse de la Serena part of any protected natural areas?
A: Yes, the Embalse de la Serena area is part of the La Siberia Biosphere Reserve, underscoring its ecological importance. Additionally, Cerro Masatrigo is a protected natural monument, included in the Natura 2000 network and designated a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA).

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while mountain biking?
A: The region is an ideal spot for birdwatching, particularly for aquatic birds and birds of prey. Species such as the Great Bustard, Little Bustard, Bonelli's Eagle, and various storks can be observed here. The characteristic vegetation includes holm oaks, cork oaks, and various scrubland species.
tips:
The castle sits atop a hill in Puebla de Alcocer (Badajoz, Extremadura), approximately 550 meters above sea level.
Its origins date back to the 12th and 13th centuries, possibly built upon the site of an earlier Mudéjar fortress.
It was remodeled in 1445 after being donated to Don Gutierre de Sotomayor, Master of the Order of Alcántara, by King John II of Castile.
At the end of the 15th century, it passed to Doña Elvira de Zúñiga, as reflected in the coat of arms on the keep.

It is worth stopping for a moment to enjoy the wonderful views that the Masatrigo hill offers us from the road. A curious shape (reminiscent of a pile of wheat) that rises above the La Serena Reservoir
Cerro Masatrigo is so called because it looks like a pile of wheat, it is located in the La Serena reservoir, it is located at an altitude of about 500 meters and with views that are worth it.
